# LinkedIn Draft - 2026-06-14

Pyodide 314.0 supports PEP 783 packaging, native ES modules, and experimental Node socket support.

Went through 40 links today. Here's what made the brief:

1. Pyodide 314.0: Python packages can now publish WebAssembly wheels to PyPI
   This materially changes Python-in-the-browser packaging and reduces friction for shipping browser Python dependencies.

2. Show HN: Paca – Lightweight Jira alternative for human-AI collaboration
   Worth watching if you care about agent-native planning tools instead of bolting chat onto existing ticket systems.

3. Show HN: Lightweight Task queue on Erlang/OTP, SQLite-backed, no overengineering
   Concrete infra tool for small teams that need durability and polyglot workers without running a full broker stack.

4. Phoenix LiveView 1.2 Released
   Framework release with concrete template/CSS workflow changes for teams shipping LiveView apps.

5. Free SQL→ER diagram tool, runs in the browser, nothing uploaded
   Small but practical utility if you regularly need quick schema visualization without pasting SQL into a hosted service.

Full brief with all 40 researched links → https://thenightlylibrarian.com/briefs/2026-06-14
